questions.question: A man of mass $70\text{ kg}$ stands on two feet. The total contact area of his feet is $0.035\text{ m}^2$. Calculate the pressure he exerts on the floor. (Take $g = 10\text{ N/kg}$)
questions.explanation:
First, find weight: $W = mg = 70 \times 10 = 700\text{ N}$. Then $p = F / A = 700 / 0.035 = 20000\text{ Pa}$. 

**Common mistake:** Using the mass ($70$) directly in the pressure formula without converting it to weight ($N$).

questions.question: A girl stands on the floor. How does the pressure she exerts change when she lifts one foot off the ground?
questions.explanation:
When she lifts one foot, the contact area $A$ is halved. Since $p = F/A$ and force (weight) is constant, halving the area doubles the pressure. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking the pressure stays the same because her weight hasn't changed.

questions.question: An object exerts a pressure of $50000\text{ Pa}$ with a force of $800\text{ N}$. Calculate the area of contact.
questions.explanation:
Rearranging $p = F/A$ gives $A = F/p$. $A = 800 / 50000 = 0.016\text{ m}^2$. 

**Common mistake:** Multiplying the pressure and force together ($40$ million).

questions.question: A force of $1.0\text{ N}$ acts on an area of $1.0\text{ cm}^2$. Convert this pressure to pascals ($Pa$).
questions.explanation:
$1\text{ cm}^2 = 0.0001\text{ m}^2$. Thus, $p = 1\text{ N} / 0.0001\text{ m}^2 = 10000\text{ Pa}$. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking $1\text{ cm}^2$ is equal to $0.01\text{ m}^2$ or $1\text{ m}^2$.

questions.question: In Table 7.1, Row 2 shows a pressure of $2000\text{ Pa}$ caused by a force of $0.08\text{ N}$. What is the area used in this case?
questions.explanation:
$A = F / p = 0.08 / 2000 = 0.00004\text{ m}^2$. 

**Common mistake:** Misplacing the decimal point during division.
